prompt:
Add Playwright e2e tests to the Balance app. Read CLAUDE.md and docs/PLAN.md for context.

CRITICAL: This is a NixOS dev environment. Playwright's downloaded Chromium WILL NOT work here because of dynamic linking. DO NOT try to run tests locally. Instead:
1. Write the tests and config
2. Verify lint and build pass (npm run lint && npm run build)
3. Trust that tests will run in CI (GitHub Actions on Ubuntu where Playwright works fine)
4. Create the PR and let CI validate the tests

## Setup

1. Install deps:
   npm install -D @playwright/test -w client

2. Create client/playwright.config.ts:
   - Use chromium only
   - baseURL: http://localhost:5173
   - Configure webServer to run 'npm run dev -w client' from root dir
   - screenshots: 'on' (capture every test)
   - video: 'retain-on-failure'
   - testDir: 'e2e'
   - Mobile viewport: 390x844
   - Set retries: 0

3. Add scripts to client/package.json:
   "test:e2e": "playwright test"

4. Add to root package.json:
   "test:e2e": "npm run test:e2e -w client"

5. Add to .gitignore: test-results/, playwright-report/, playwright/.cache/

## Test Files (client/e2e/)

IMPORTANT: Read client/src/store.ts FIRST to understand the exact metric IDs and names used in the default metrics. Read client/src/components/MetricSlider.tsx and client/src/App.tsx to understand the DOM structure and any data-testid attributes. Write tests that match the ACTUAL code.

### app-loads.spec.ts
- Navigate to /
- Wait for app to load (the metrics list should be visible)
- Verify 'Balance' text appears in the header
- Verify all 11 default metric names are visible (get the exact names from store.ts)
- Take a full page screenshot

### metric-recording.spec.ts  
- Navigate to /
- Find a metric slider (use the ACTUAL metric name from store.ts, e.g. first metric)
- Click the 3rd step button on it
- Verify the step shows as selected (has the active styling - check MetricSlider.tsx for the CSS classes)
- Verify 'Recorded' text appears for that metric
- Take screenshot after recording
- Reload the page
- Verify the value persists (IndexedDB survives reload)
- Take screenshot after reload showing persisted value

### pwa.spec.ts
- Fetch /manifest.webmanifest and verify it returns valid JSON with name 'Balance'
- Check that the page has a theme-color meta tag
- Take screenshot

## CI Integration

Read the existing .github/workflows/ci.yml and ADD a new job called 'e2e' that:
- runs-on: ubuntu-latest
- Steps:
  1. Checkout code
  2. Setup Node.js (match the version from the existing build job)
  3. npm ci
  4. npx playwright install chromium --with-deps
  5. npm run test:e2e
  6. Upload test-results/ as artifact (use actions/upload-artifact@v4), always() condition so screenshots are captured even on failure

## Add data-testid attributes

If the components don't have good selectors, add minimal data-testid attributes:
- MetricSlider: data-testid="metric-{id}" on the container
- Step buttons: data-testid="step-{index}" on each clickable step
- Header: data-testid="app-header"

## Verification (local)

Since Playwright can't run on NixOS, only verify:
- npm run lint passes
- npm run build passes
- The test files have no TypeScript errors

Create PR titled 'Add Playwright e2e tests with screenshot capture'
